questions.question: The committee, ---- by a diverse group of experts, has put forth a comprehensive report on sustainable urban development.
questions.solution: 'Comprising' here functions as a participle modifying 'committee'. 'Composed of' is also grammatically correct but less concise in this context. 'Which comprises' would require a relative clause structure.

questions.question: By the time the rescue team arrived, the hikers ---- for over 24 hours, battling harsh weather conditions.
questions.solution: The past perfect continuous tense ('had been losing') is used to emphasize the duration of an action that was completed before another past action (the arrival of the rescue team).

questions.question: Had the company ---- its security protocols more rigorously, the data breach might have been averted.
questions.solution: This is a third conditional sentence with inversion. The structure 'Had + subject + past participle' is used to express a hypothetical past condition.

questions.question: It is imperative that all employees ---- their annual performance reviews by the end of the fiscal year.
questions.solution: The subjunctive mood is required after 'imperative that'. The base form of the verb ('complete') is used regardless of the subject.

questions.question: Despite the initial setbacks, the ambitious project ---- by a dedicated team of engineers and scientists.
questions.solution: The present continuous passive ('is being seen through') indicates an ongoing action that is happening now or around now, fitting the context of a project currently in progress despite challenges.
